bugs.title: Shared view itinerary count differs from GetItinerary
bugs.description: GetItinerary returns 22 items across 7 days, but the shared view returns 20 items across 7 days. Two items appear to be dropped in the shared view projection. Day 6 shows 2 items in shared view vs potentially more via GetItinerary. This may be due to items created in a second tool call batch not being included, or a serialization discrepancy.
bugs.expected: 22 itinerary items in shared view (matching GetItinerary)

bugs.title: Usage counter shows 1 but 2 AI messages were sent
bugs.description: Two SendMessage calls were made (one in selection mode, one in planning mode), but GET /api/usage returns used:1. The selection-mode message that created the trip may not be counting toward usage.

ux_issues.description: Shared view booking details only include type, title, and provider. Confirmation code, dates, and address are omitted. While this is safer from a privacy standpoint, it limits the usefulness of shared trip views for collaborators who need booking details.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider including non-sensitive booking metadata (check-in/out dates, confirmation code) in the shared view, while continuing to omit raw text and guest personal details.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The sharing lifecycle works correctly and securely. Token revocation properly invalidates old tokens, re-sharing generates new unique tokens, and old tokens remain invalid after re-share. The shared view contains meaningful trip content (itinerary with 20 items across 7 days, booking info) without leaking private data (no userId, email, or JWT tokens). The itinerary quality was excellent with well-organized day-by-day plans covering Tokyo, Hakone, and Kyoto. Booking parsing correctly extracted all hotel details from the ryokan confirmation.
